heading: Sixto QA/RT landing tests now rerun on the corrected CCH witness using G, blue wedge edges, and vertical proxies
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto-ramos, corrected-witness, rational-trig, geometry-test]
body: Rebuilt `sixto_cch_frame_map.json` and `sixto_qa_rt_landing_tests.json` after switching the active baseline from the old merged red-center packet to `cch_archm1b_labeled_witness.json`. The active witness packet is now `G=(1625,1305)`, blue rising edge `(1409,1016)->(1785,1516)`, blue falling edge `(1790,1508)->(2243,1167)`, and central vertical proxies at `x=1368/1372`. Honest rerun verdict: still no nontrivial exact QA/RT landing on the recovered geometry. The closest hits are source-level near-incidences, not exact equalities: `G` to rising-edge residual `1.06138`, left-shoulder to rising-edge residual `0.856777`, far-apex to falling-edge residual `0.368606`, lower-apex to falling-edge residual `0.395061`, and blue-edge spread `0.999999764877` with `delta_from_one = -2.35123e-07`. The witness triangles also remain non-Pythagorean at exact quadrance level: for example `(G, blue_far_apex, blue_lower_apex)` gives quadrances `380945, 68290, 298553` with obstruction `14102`. Historical stage-center maps are now retained only as comparison context and no longer count toward the active verdict.
